title: How Sinners Oscar-Nominated Editor Michael P. Shawver Carved Ryan Coogler's Beautiful Chaos Into Pure Cinema
text: Oscar-nominated editor Michael P. Shawver transformed Ryan Coogler's ambitious vampire thriller Sinners into a cohesive cinematic experience. Shawver has collaborated with Coogler on every film since their USC meeting, establishing a deep creative partnership. Shawver traveled to Louisiana to edit during production, addressing the unique challenges of filming on celluloid in unpredictable bayou conditions. He worked beneath a tent with his Avid setup, monitoring footage and consulting with Coogler about shots and takes. Film has a different relationship with light, Shawver explained, noting the team had to make immediate decisions rather than waiting for the edit room. Shawver strategically withheld visual information to heighten tension. When the character Cornbread encounters danger, Shawver removed a visual signal originally in the script, noting that if the audience doesn't know, it's better. The centerpiece musical scene required assembling hundreds of shots to match the exact song length. Shawver used spiritual matches—both action and emotional—to build fear and anticipation. During a demanding shooting schedule, Coogler requested Shawver create a 5-10 minute sizzle reel by 6 p.m., which energized the exhausted crew.
